us-politics.headline: U.S. Fighter Jet Shot Down Over Iran, One Crew Member Missing
us-politics.body:
A U.S. fighter jet was shot down over Iran, with one crew member rescued and a search underway for a second. The incident marks a sharp escalation in tensions with Tehran and comes as Trump's wartime rhetoric has drawn scrutiny for departing from the measured language used by past presidents. Critics argue the tone raises the risk of miscalculation; supporters say it projects strength.

NPR notes Trump's framing of military action differs from predecessors in scope and directness. The downing of the aircraft puts that rhetoric to a concrete test, with real consequences for personnel and diplomacy in a region where roughly half of Americans already expect stability to worsen, according to a recent survey.

sources.title: How Trump's wartime rhetoric differs from past presidents and why that matters
President Trump's rocky week at home and abroad
About half expect worsening stability in Middle East: Survey
1 rescued after U.S. fighter jet shot down in Iran; search ongoing for crew member

us-politics.headline: Justice Alito Hospitalised in March; Court Kept It Quiet
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court confirmed Justice Samuel Alito fell ill at a March 20 event and was treated for dehydration at a hospital. The court did not disclose the incident publicly at the time. Alito has since returned to his duties.

The delayed disclosure has drawn attention given the court's role in high-stakes cases currently before it. The court offered no explanation for why the hospitalisation was not announced sooner. Alito's health had not previously been reported as a concern.

sources.title: Justice Alito ‘felt ill’ at March event, was examined by doctor, court says
Supreme Court confirms Alito fell ill at March event
Supreme Court Justice Alito treated for dehydration at hospital in previously undisclosed March 20 incident

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Targets TSA, Alcatraz, and Blue-State Fraud
us-politics.body:
Trump's budget proposal would cut TSA funding and require small airports to privatise security screening, raising concerns about wait times and safety standards at regional hubs. Democrats called the broader budget request bleak and unacceptable, pointing to cuts across multiple agencies. Republicans framed the reductions as necessary fiscal discipline.

The same budget seeks $152 million to reopen Alcatraz as a high-security federal prison, a proposal Trump has championed publicly. Separately, Vice President Vance has been designated to lead a fraud task force targeting what the White House describes as taxpayer theft concentrated in Democratic-led states. Critics called that framing politically motivated.

sources.title: Trump budget would cut TSA funding, require privatization at small airports
Trump seeks $152M to rebuild Alcatraz as ‘state-of-the-art secure prison’
Airport security: Are TSA wait times still long? Here’s how to check
Democrats fume at White House budget request: ‘Bleak and unacceptable’
Vance tapped as ‘fraud czar’ as Trump targets blue states over taxpayer theft

us-politics.headline: DOJ Shake-Up, Epstein Files, and ActBlue Under Fire
us-politics.body:
Todd Blanche, Trump's former personal attorney, has taken over as the acting head of the Justice Department following Pam Bondi's departure as attorney general. The transition puts a loyalist in one of the most powerful legal offices in the country at a moment of intense scrutiny over DOJ priorities. Fox News reports several contenders are being considered to replace Bondi permanently.

On Capitol Hill, Rep. Nancy Mace is pressing for Bondi to testify about the Epstein files before she leaves office, saying she is not backing down. Separately, Democratic fundraising platform ActBlue faces allegations it misled Congress about foreign donations, a charge the platform disputes. The FBI also notified Congress of a major data breach in a surveillance system.

sources.title: Mace ‘not backing away’ from Bondi testifying on Epstein
Todd Blanche takes on DOJ’s top job and the burden of Trump’s expectations
FBI labels data breach ‘major incident,’ notifies Congress
Pam Bondi is out as AG — here are the contenders who could replace her
Dem fundraising giant ActBlue rocked by allegations it misled Congress about foreign donations

au-politics.headline: Iran War Reshapes Australian Politics and Global Alliances
au-politics.body:
The US-Iran conflict is rippling through Australian domestic politics, with Prime Minister Albanese facing pressure to respond to a rapidly shifting strategic environment. The Guardian reports the crisis has prompted Albanese to consider bolder economic reforms, while world leaders including Macron and Merz are growing openly impatient with Trump's handling of the war.

The conflict's economic fallout is landing hard in Australia's region. The Philippines is sliding toward an energy crisis as gas supplies tighten, and a 'regime change' in the Strait of Hormuz has left 20,000 sailors stranded at sea, threatening global shipping lanes. Energy Minister Chris Bowen is under pressure to explain the domestic fuel impact.

On tax, both Albanese and opposition leader Taylor are scrambling to match voter expectations, with even Liberal MP Andrew Hastie suggesting miners could pay more.

sources.title: World loses patience with Trump as war becomes about more than Iran
Live: US crew member rescued after fighter jet downed over Iran
How the Iran war is pushing US ally the Philippines into economic crisis
'Regime change' in the Strait of Hormuz sees 20,000 sailors stranded at sea
Energy minister Chris Bowen on the impact of the fuel crisis - podcast
Taxing times for Albanese and Taylor as parties seek to match voter expectations
Trump’s Iran war has woken Albanese up to a new reality. Will it spur him towards ‘ambitious’ reforms?

au-politics.headline: US Military Expansion in Pacific Divides Local Communities
au-politics.body:
Guam is at the centre of a deepening debate over America's military build-up in the Pacific. Residents are split - some see the expanded US presence as essential security, others view it as a threat to sovereignty and daily life. The tension reflects broader anxiety across the region as great-power competition intensifies.

The story sits alongside Australia's own strategic recalibration, with the Iran war accelerating questions about defence posture, alliance obligations, and economic resilience. How far Australia aligns with US military priorities in the Pacific remains a live political question with no clear consensus.

sources.title: Strategically critical Pacific island divided over growing US military presence
World loses patience with Trump as war becomes about more than Iran
Trump’s Iran war has woken Albanese up to a new reality. Will it spur him towards ‘ambitious’ reforms?

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Narelle Leaves Trail of Destruction Across Australia
au-politics.body:
Cyclone Narelle carved a damaging path across Australia, with ABC publishing detailed visual documentation of the storm's progression. The coverage pieced together the cyclone's track frame by frame, illustrating the scale of destruction left in its wake.

The event adds pressure on governments managing compounding crises - a fuel shortage, global instability, and now a significant natural disaster. Emergency response and infrastructure resilience will face scrutiny in the aftermath.

sources.title: The destructive path of Cyclone Narelle, pieced together frame by frame

au-politics.headline: Queensland Man Jailed for Streaming Child Abuse Online
au-politics.body:
A Queensland man has been sentenced to 15 years in prison after live-streaming child sexual abuse. The North Lakes man was convicted on multiple child abuse offences in a case that drew attention to the ongoing threat of online exploitation.

The sentence reflects courts' increasingly firm approach to crimes involving the live distribution of abuse material, which investigators say complicates detection and intervention compared to static offending.

sources.title: Queensland man jailed for 15 years after live-streaming child abuse

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Jets Downed, UAE Attacked
world.body: Iran claims it shot down two US fighter jets, with search operations underway for at least one pilot still missing. Tehran released footage of US aircraft conducting search missions over Iranian territory, while drone footage showed an Israeli factory damaged by Iranian missile debris. The conflict is spreading regionally. A strike on the UAE's Habshan gas facility killed at least one person, and the WHO reports more than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1, including a Red Crescent supply warehouse. A French-owned container ship became the first commercial vessel to transit the Strait of Hormuz since the war began, a closely watched signal for global shipping. Trump, meanwhile, suggested the US could eventually "take the oil" in Iran.

sources.title: More than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1: WHO
At least one killed at UAE’s Habshan gas facility after intercepted attack
What we know about the US fighter jet shot down in Iran
French-owned container ship transits Hormuz Strait in first since Iran war
Drone footage shows a factory in Israel damaged by Iranian missile debris
Iran says it downed two US jets as search for one pilot continues
Trump says with more time, US can ‘take the oil’ in Iran
Red Crescent supply warehouse attacked in southwest Iran
Iran release videos of US aircraft searching for pilots shot down in Iran

world.headline: Trump Seeks $1.5 Trillion Defence Budget Amid Legal Battles
world.body: The White House submitted a record $1.5 trillion defence budget request to Congress, pairing the military spending surge with deep cuts to domestic programmes. The proposal arrives as the administration fights legal challenges on multiple fronts. Two dozen Democrat-led states filed suit over Trump's restrictions on mail-in voting, and a federal judge upheld a ruling tossing subpoenas targeting Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, blocking attempts to pressure the central bank. The budget request reflects a wartime posture, coming as US forces are actively engaged in Iran and military spending faces intense scrutiny on Capitol Hill.

sources.title: Two dozen Democrat-led states sue Trump over mail-in ballot limits
US judge upholds decision to toss subpoenas into Fed Chair Jerome Powell
Trump seeks historic $1.5 trillion for military in Congress budget request
Trump seeks $1.5tn for defence alongside domestic spending cuts

world.headline: Sudan Hospital Strike Kills 10; Ukraine Claims Battlefield Gains
world.body: A drone strike hit a hospital in Sudan, killing at least 10 people, according to medical charity MSF. The attack is among the deadliest single strikes on civilian infrastructure in the ongoing Sudanese conflict. Separately, Ukraine reported slowing Russian advances and reclaiming territory, while claiming its operations are draining Moscow's war finances. The battlefield update offers Kyiv a rare moment of momentum, though the broader war remains grinding and attritional.

sources.title: Drone strike on Sudan hospital kills 10, medical charity MSF says
Ukraine slows enemy advances, liberates land, drains Russia’s war chest

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Photographs Earth; Cuba to Free 2,000 Prisoners
world.body: NASA's Artemis II crew captured striking images of Earth during their lunar mission, offering a rare human perspective from deep space. On the ground, Cuba announced it will release more than 2,000 prisoners as US diplomatic pressure intensifies. The move signals a potential shift in Havana's posture, though analysts remain cautious about reading it as a broader political opening. Cambodia's parliament also passed new legislation targeting cybercrime scam networks, which have victimised thousands across Southeast Asia.

sources.title: Artemis II crew take 'spectacular' image of Earth
Cambodia parliament approves law to combat cybercrime scam rings
Cuba to release more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts

ai.headline: AI's Energy Hunger Drives Gas Plant Boom, Neighbors Push Back
ai.body:
Microsoft, Meta, and Google are commissioning new natural gas plants to feed data center demand, betting that AI's growth justifies locking in fossil fuel infrastructure for decades. Critics warn the buildout undermines climate commitments and hands utilities leverage over tech companies desperate for power at any cost.

Local opposition is stiffening. A new survey finds Americans would sooner accept an Amazon warehouse next door than a data center, citing noise, water use, and grid strain. The gap between industry promises and community tolerance is widening fast, and regulators in several states are starting to pay attention.

sources.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center

ai.headline: OpenAI Buys a Talk Show While Its Executives Exit
ai.body:
OpenAI acquired TBPN, a tech-focused talk show, adding a media property to a portfolio that already stretches well beyond AI research. The purchase signals a push to control narrative and distribution, not just build models.

Internally, the company is less stable. Fidji Simo, hired less than a year ago to run OpenAI's applications business, is taking medical leave amid a broader executive shake-up. The timing raises questions about leadership continuity as OpenAI navigates a crowded competitive landscape and ongoing restructuring.

sources.title: OpenAI takes on another "side quest," buys tech-focused talk show TBPN
OpenAI's Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up

ai.headline: Chatbots Prescribe Psychiatric Drugs as AI Enters the Clinic
ai.body:
AI chatbots are now prescribing and refilling psychiatric medications for patients, moving from wellness tools into territory that regulators have historically reserved for licensed clinicians. The Verge reports the practice is already live, not theoretical.

The risks are significant. Psychiatric drugs carry serious side-effect profiles and require monitoring that a chatbot cannot perform. Meanwhile, NHS staff in the UK are separately refusing to use Palantir's Federated Data Platform over ethical concerns, suggesting a growing divide between what health tech companies deploy and what frontline workers will actually use.

sources.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs
NHS staff refusing to use FDP over Palantir ethical concerns

ai.headline: H.264 License Fees Spike 45x, Hitting Streaming Hard
ai.body:
Via Licensing Alliance has raised H.264 streaming license fees from $100,000 to $4.5 million, a 45-fold increase that blindsided smaller streaming operators. H.264 remains the most widely used video codec on the internet, meaning few services can simply walk away.

The hike arrives as the codec licensing landscape grows more contentious. Operators now face a stark choice: absorb costs, accelerate migration to royalty-free alternatives like AV1, or pass fees on to subscribers. An Italian court this week also ordered Netflix to refund customers up to 500 euros for unlawful price hikes, adding pressure on streaming economics from multiple directions.

sources.title: Firm boosts H.264 streaming license fees from $100k up to staggering $4.5M
Netflix must refund customers for years of price hikes, Italian court rules

btc.headline: Circle Faces Backlash After $285M Drift Hack Goes Unfrozen
btc.body:
Circle is under mounting pressure after refusing to freeze USDC linked to a $285 million exploit of the Drift protocol. On-chain investigator ZachXBT escalated the criticism, accusing Circle of over $420 million in compliance failures since 2022, arguing the stablecoin issuer has repeatedly failed to act against illicit flows.

Drift attempted its own workaround, sending an on-chain message directly to wallets tied to the hack in a bid to open negotiations with the attacker. The episode sharpens a long-running debate about whether centralized stablecoin issuers should wield their freeze powers more aggressively, and what it means for trust in USDC when they don't.

sources.title: Circle under fire after $285 million Drift hack over inaction to freeze stolen USDC
ZachXBT accuses Circle of $420M in 'compliance failures' since 2022
Drift sends onchain message to wallets tied to $280M exploit

btc.headline: Schwab Eyes Spot Crypto Trading as Bitcoin Dips Below $70K
btc.body:
Charles Schwab plans to launch direct spot trading for Bitcoin and Ether in the first half of 2026, a move that would bring one of America's largest retail brokerages fully into the crypto market. The announcement lands as Bitcoin trades flat near the $70,000 level, with futures markets tilting bearish and volatility cooling across the board.

Altcoins are showing modest relative strength, with XRP climbing to $1.33 before stalling at resistance. Cathie Wood, meanwhile, told investors that Bitcoin's era of 85% drawdowns is over, though she set a near-term target of $34,000 - well below current prices. Corporate Bitcoin treasury strategies are also diverging, with some firms holding firm while others reassess as BTC softens.

sources.title: Schwab plans spot bitcoin, ether trading launch in first half of 2026
What next as XRP rises to $1.33 but fails to break out
Crypto Biz: Bitcoin treasuries break ranks as BTC dips below $70K
CoinDesk 20 performance update: Bitcoin (BTC) trades flat while altcoins rise
Bitcoin 'done' with 85% crashes, says Cathie Wood amid new $34K target
Crypto consolidates as volatility cools and futures markets tilt bearish

btc.headline: Coinbase Trust Charter Draws Fire From Community Banks
btc.body:
US community banks are pushing back against the OCC's approval of a federal trust charter for Coinbase, arguing the move gives a crypto firm access to banking infrastructure without the same regulatory burden traditional lenders carry. The opposition reflects a broader turf war as crypto companies pursue bank-like licenses that could let them custody assets and offer financial services at scale.

The fight mirrors tensions in Europe, where Malta is clashing with ESMA over centralized crypto oversight under MiCA. Both disputes point to the same underlying friction: regulators and incumbents are struggling to define where crypto firms fit in a financial system that wasn't built for them.

sources.title: US community banks oppose OCC's approval of Coinbase trust charter
Centralizing crypto: Why Malta’s clash with ESMA is about more than one small state

btc.headline: Ethereum Foundation Stakes $93M as L2 Scaling Debate Heats Up
btc.body:
The Ethereum Foundation completed a staking milestone, depositing another $93 million in ETH to hit its 70,000 ETH target. The move signals a more active treasury posture from a body that has historically kept its holdings largely liquid.

Offchain Labs separately argued that Ethereum Layer 2 networks need dynamic, responsive fee pricing to handle the transaction volumes required for mainstream scale. The technical push comes as Binance retained its lead in crypto derivatives through Q1, while Hyperliquid broke into the top 10 for the first time - a sign that decentralized perpetuals are gaining serious ground on centralized venues.

sources.title: Ethereum Foundation stakes another $93 million ether, reaching its 70,000 ETH target
Ethereum L2s need responsive pricing to scale, says Offchain Labs
Binance led Q1 crypto derivatives as Hyperliquid cracked top 10: CoinGlass

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, buttery cod fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until caramelised and lacquered
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: The Deck of Cards Workout
exercise.equipment: One standard deck of playing cards, exercise mat
exercise.description: Assign a bodyweight move to each suit — push-ups, squats, lunges, plank holds — then flip cards one by one and perform the reps shown, turning a random deck into an unpredictable full-body circuit

learn.description: Stochastic resonance is the counterintuitive phenomenon where adding a precise amount of random noise to a weak signal actually makes that signal easier to detect — used in neuroscience, physics, and even hearing aid design
learn.why: It quietly dismantles the assumption that noise is always the enemy of clarity, and once you understand it you start spotting its fingerprints everywhere from how neurons fire to how financial markets move
